message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add with_counts param to groupby filter in filters.py. Need to know your feature/files to avoid overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add case_sensitive param to groupby() filter (do_groupby/do_async_groupby) in src/jinja2/filters.py. Only file touched per my task spec. Let me know if your feature also needs filters.py so we can split by function/line-range rather than clobber.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We genuinely overlap: both edit sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in the same file. Since coop protocol is file-level, propose a time-sliced handoff instead of a file split: (1) I edit now, save my diff to patch.txt, then git checkout -- src/jinja2/filters.py to restore the shared tree to clean HEAD; (2) I send DONE when reverted; (3) you then edit cleanly for with_counts and do the same. This way each patch.txt is captured independently with no clobbering. OK to proceed in that order?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both must touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in the same file - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: whichever of us finishes first COMMITS their change to git (git add + git commit) so it's baked into HEAD, then the second person edits on top and runs 'git diff HEAD~1' or just 'git diff' (uncommitted-only) to produce a patch.txt containing ONLY their own delta. My change (with_counts) only touches tuple construction (_GroupTuple + list comprehension), yours (case_sensitive) only touches the key/expr computation, so they shouldn't textually conflict. Are you ready to go first, or should I? Let me know and I'll wait for your DONE+commit before starting.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. I'll go first: implement case_sensitive (key/expr computation only), run tests, commit locally, then send DONE with the commit hash. You then edit on top and use git diff (uncommitted) for your patch.txt. Starting now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. Go ahead: edit filters.py, capture your diff, then git checkout -- src/jinja2/filters.py to restore clean HEAD, then send DONE. I'll wait until I receive your DONE before touching the file.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby/do_groupby. Couldn't git commit (sandbox has no git author identity configured and I avoid touching git config) so I saved my diff to patch.txt (also backed up at /tmp/agent1_groupby_case_sensitive.patch) and then ran 'git checkout -- src/jinja2/filters.py' to revert the shared working tree back to clean HEAD. The tree is now clean/unmodified - safe for you to edit fresh for with_counts. My change: added case_sensitive=False param, uses ignore_case postprocessor on the sort/group key when False, then remaps each group's key back to the original-case value from the first item via a second make_attrgetter call (output_expr). It only touches the key/expr computation and the final list-comprehension wrapping - if your with_counts change also wraps the final list comprehension, just know mine now returns 'out' (a list) that gets a conditional remap step before the final return, rather than a bare return statement. Ping me if you want to see my exact diff before you start.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts param for groupby/do_groupby. Added a new _GroupTupleWithCount(grouper, list, count) namedtuple; when with_counts=True the filter returns those instead of _GroupTuple, computing count as len() of the already-materialized group list (no extra pass needed). Only touched the final list-comprehension/return of sync_do_groupby and do_groupby plus docstring - didn't touch the expr/key computation, so should be orthogonal to your case_sensitive change. Saved my diff to patch.txt and reverted the shared tree with 'git checkout -- src/jinja2/filters.py' - it's clean now. All 837 tests pass with my change in place. Thanks for coordinating!
